name: lazyfeed

metadata.name: lazyfeed
metadata.summary: lazyfeed is a fast and simple terminal base RSS/Atom reader built using textual.

documentation:
# lazyfeed

A fast, modern, and simple RSS/Atom feed reader for the terminal written in pure Python.

![Loaded screenshot](./.github/screenshot.png) ## Features - Support for RSS/Atom feeds. - Import from and export feeds. - Save for later. - Vim-like keybindings for navigation. - Theming. - "In-app" reading support. - Configuration options. ## Core dependencies - [textual](https://www.textualize.io/). - [aiohttp](https://docs.aiohttp.org/en/stable/index.html). - [feedparser](https://feedparser.readthedocs.io/en/latest/basic.html). - [sqlalchemy](https://www.sqlalchemy.org/). ## Motivation For quite some time, I have wanted to build an RSS reader for myself. While I appreciated some existing solutions, I often felt that they were missing key features or included unnecessary ones. I wanted a simple, fast, and elegant way to stay updated with my favorite feeds without the hassle of limits, ads, or cumbersome configuration files. ## Coming up - Full-text search support. - Categories. - Better in-app reading experience. - Customizable keybindings. ## Installation The recommended way is by using [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/tools/): ```bash uv tool install --python 3.13 lazyfeed ``` Now you just need to import your feeds from an OPML file like this: ```bash lazyfeed < ~/Downloads/feeds.opml ``` Or, after starting `lazyfeed`, adding your favorite feeds one by one: ![Add feed](./.github/screenshot_add_feed.png) ## Import and export As you can see importing your RSS feeds is pretty simple and exporting them is as simple as just doing: ```bash lazyfeed > ~/Downloads/feeds.opml ``` > At the moment only OPML is supported. ## Configuration The configuration file for `lazyfeed` is located at `$XSG_CONFIG_HOME/lazyfeed/config.toml`. This file is generated automatically the first time you run `lazyfeed` and will look something like this: ```toml # Welcome! This is the configuration file for lazyfeed. # Available themes include: # - "dracula" # - "textual-dark" # - "textual-light" # - "nord" # - "gruvbox" # - "catppuccin-mocha" # - "textual-ansi" # - "tokyo-night" # - "monokai" # - "flexoki" # - "catppuccin-latte" # - "solarized-light" theme = "dracula" # If set to true, all items will be marked as read when quitting the application. auto_read = false # If set to true, items will be fetched at start. auto_load = false # If set to false, items will be marked as read without asking for confirmation. confirm_before_read = true # Specifies by which attribute the items will be sorted. sort_by = "published_at" # "title", "is_read", "published_at" # Specifies the sort order. sort_order = "ascending" # "descending", "ascending" [client] # Maximum times (in seconds) to wait for all request operations. timeout = 300 # Timeout for establishing a connection. connect_timeout = 10 [client.headers] # This section defines the HTTP headers that will be sent with # each request. # User-Agent = "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/128.0.0.0 Safari/537.36" # Accept = "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8" # Accept-Language = "en-US,en;q=0.6" # Accept-Encoding = "gzip,deflate,br,zstd" ``` > The folder that holds the configuration file as well as the SQLite database is determined by the `get_app_dir` utility provided by `click`. You can read more about it [here](https://click.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/api/#click.get_app_dir). ## Usage To start using `lazyfeed` you just need to run: ```bash lazyfeed ``` ## Some screenshots ![Confirmation](./.github/screenshot_confirmation.png) ![In-app reader](./.github/screenshot_in_app_reader.png) ![Saved for later](./.github/screenshot_save_for_later.png) > The theme used for the screenshots is `dracula`.

functions.name: lazyfeed.main.fetch_new_feeds
documentation: Fetch and store new RSS feeds.

functions.name: lazyfeed.feeds.clean_html
documentation:
Removes unwanted content from the given HTML.

Args:
    html (str): The HTML content to clean.

Returns:
    str | None: The cleaned HTML content, or None in the case of error.

functions.name: lazyfeed.feeds.fetch_feed
documentation:
Fetch and parse an RSS feed from the specified URL.

Args:
    client (aiohttp.ClientSession): The HTTP client session to use for the request.
    url (str): The URL of the feed to be fetched.
    title (str | None): Optional title for the feed.

Returns:
    Feed: Feed object.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If the feed cannot be fetched or is badly formatted.

functions.name: lazyfeed.feeds.fetch_entries
documentation:
Fetch entries from the specified RSS feed URL.

Args:
    client (aiohttp.ClientSession): The HTTP client session to use for the request.
    url (str): The URL of the feed to fetch entries from.
    etag (str): Optional ETag header to check if the feed has been updates since the last time.

Returns:
    tuple[list[dict], str]: A tuple containing a list of entries and the ETag from the response.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If the feed cannot be fetched or is badly formatted.

functions.name: lazyfeed.feeds.fetch_content
documentation:
Fetch and parse the content of a specific entry.

Args:
    client (aiohttp.ClientSession): The HTTP client session to use for the request.
    entry_data (dict): The data of the entry to fetch content for.
    feed_id (int): The ID of the feed associated with the entry.

Returns:
    Item | None: An Item object containing the entry's content, or None if the entry is invalid or something went wrong while parsing.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If the content cannot be fetched.

functions.name: lazyfeed.utils.export_opml
documentation: Export a list of RSS feeds into an OPML formatted string.

functions.name: lazyfeed.utils.import_opml
documentation: Import RSS feeds from an OPML formatted string.

functions.name: lazyfeed.http_client.http_client_session
documentation:
Asynchronous context manager for creating an HTTP client session using
aiohttp.ClientSession. It configures the session with specified timeouts
and headers from the provided settings, and the session is automatically
closed upon exiting the context.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.AddFeed
documentation: Message to add a new RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.EditFeed
documentation: Message to edit an existing RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.DeleteFeed
documentation: Message to delete a specified RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.FilterByFeed
documentation: Message to filter by the specified RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.MarkAsRead
documentation: Message to mark an item as 'read'.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.MarkAllAsRead
documentation: Message to mark all items as 'read'.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.Open
documentation: Message to open item's content.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.OpenInBrowser
documentation: Message to open an item in the browser.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.SaveForLater
documentation: Message to save an item for later.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.ShowPending
documentation: Message to list all items.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.ShowAll
documentation: Message to list all pending items.

classes.name: lazyfeed.messages.ShowSavedForLater
documentation: Message to list all saved for later items.

functions.name: lazyfeed.db.init_db
documentation:
Initialize database by creating all tables defined in the
ORM models.

functions.name: lazyfeed.widgets.validators.is_valid_url
documentation:
Check if the provided string is a valid URL.

Args:
    value (str): The string to be validated.

Returns:
    bool: True if the string is a valid URL, False otherwise.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.item_table.ItemTable
documentation: Custom DataTable widget for displaying and managing a list of items.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.modals.help_modal.HelpModal
documentation: Modal screen to display help information for a specific widget.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.modals.edit_feed_modal.EditFeedModal
documentation: Modal screen for editing an existing RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.modals.confirm_action_modal.ConfirmActionModal
documentation: Modal screen with a prompt for confirmation of an action.

classes.name: lazyfeed.app.LazyFeedApp
documentation: A simple and modern RSS feed reader for the terminal.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.helpable.HelpData
documentation: Data related to a widget to be displayed in the help modal.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.helpable.Helpable
documentation:
Protocol for widgets that contain information required by the
help modal.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.custom_header.CustomHeader
documentation: Custom header widget displaying a title and subtitle.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.modals.add_feed_modal.AddFeedModal
documentation: Modal screen for adding a new RSS feed.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.item_screen.ItemScreen
documentation: Screen for displaying the content of a single item in markdown format.

functions.name: lazyfeed.decorators.fetch_guard
documentation:
Decorator to prevent multiple fetch request at the same time and
avoid executing certain actions while a fetch is in progress.

functions.name: lazyfeed.decorators.rollback_session
documentation:
Decorator to handle exceptions and perform a rollback if needed. It also
notifies the user with an error message and, if specified, executes a callback
function at the end.

classes.name: lazyfeed.widgets.rss_feed_tree.RSSFeedTree
documentation:
Custom Tree that provides functionality to show, add, edit, delete and filter
by feeds.
